insight: Holding Arka (and similar absorb units like Omega, Doomed Mech) is usually superior to clicking it for damage, because it enables you to buy significantly less defense that turn (gaining 7 defense tempo by holding vs. losing 7 defense by clicking), whereas the opponent only loses 4 defense half a turn later if you click. This tempo advantage compounds as inflation increases.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of pro replay where Arka holding decision was debated between liadahlia, awaclus, and others.

insight: When you have both Odin and Splitter, the best play is usually to hold Odin and click the Splitter, because clicking Odin costs +1 absorb and 4 defensive supply in addition to the Splitter cost, making it worse than just clicking Splitter.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of optimal click patterns for Odin in early game.

insight: In breach decisions between Vivid Drone and Tarsier: breach Tarsier over Vivid in high inflation (where the soak value of Vivid is less critical), but breach Vivid over Tarsier in low inflation. However, Vivid is the safer 99% answer because if breaching Vivid is correct you can still win by breaching Tarsier, but not vice versa.
author: mrguy888
context: Extended discussion about breach priorities between two units.

insight: Resource production units (Blastforge, Animus, Conduit) are approximately balanced to produce around 1/3 of their gold cost in value per turn. This derived from Drone costing 3 gold and producing 1 gold per turn serves as the baseline for valuing other resources at roughly: Red = 1 gold, Green ≈ 1.33 gold, Blue ≈ 1.67 gold.
author: consensus
context: Lengthy technical discussion about resource value conversion and balance model assumptions.

insight: For high-econ P2 openings with Vivids: the sequence 1EE 1DD DDDD is stronger than DD DDEE by approximately 1 gold.
author: mtanzer
context: Brief note on vivid-set high econ opening efficiency.

insight: Doomed Drones can be used as efficient economy in early game by letting them die to damage or filling available supply, effectively functioning as better Engineers. Do not force them to die on specific turns just to accumulate gold.
author: 1m2o
context: Discussion of high-econ P2 openings and when to use Doomed Drones.

insight: Odin and Arka work well together as a rush because clicking Odin immediately gives +7 attack, and then buying Arka afterward allows you to hold it for defense tempo. However, this requires an Animus setup to afford Arka immediately after Odin, which is difficult if you already spent resources on BB for the Odin buy.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of Odin+Arka synergy and practical deckbuilding constraints.

insight: When an opponent has Frostbites, you should sacrifice Doomed Walls before they reach 1 lifespan remaining, because Frostbites will trigger on that final turn and you lose the soak value.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion of unit lifespan management against Frostbite.

insight: Holding absorb units (Arka, Omega, Doomed Mech) is less imperative when you can click them in lower inflation games, or when both players are mutually breaching. Clicking Omega is less bad than clicking Arka because Omega's absorb value per turn is lower.
author: Deleted User, kristian1103
context: Nuance discussion on when holding absorb units is forced vs. optional.

insight: Centurion absorb (gaining +1 value per turn) is almost always taken when available, as the only exceptions are rare niche cases (e.g., Vai Mauronax sets where you defer Centurion, or Thorium/Endotherm Kit sets). The default advice should reflect the 99% case.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of when to absorb on Centurion vs. leaving it unabsorbed.

insight: Never click a Pixie if your opponent has Plexo Cell and you have no other damage, as Plexo Cell's 1-damage-barrier completely negates Pixie's small attack.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of hardline rules in Prismata that are never broken.

insight: Amporilla requires sufficient support (big absorbers like Matrix, or red sinks like Perforator/Chrono Filter/Fugue) and does not work as a high-econ build. Optimal Amporilla builds typically start at 4 Tarsiers on ~13 drones and scale up to 5-6 Tarsiers depending on the quality of supporting units.
author: mtanzer, amalloy
context: Discussion of when and how to build Amporilla in different unit sets.

insight: In Prismata notation, lowercase letters (r, g, b) denote resource types (red, green, blue) and uppercase letters (R, G, B) denote units that produce those resources. Gold is typically written with a standalone number (e.g., '2' means 2 gold), and can optionally be labeled as '2 gold' for clarity to new players. Capital G is reserved for Gaussite.
author: mrguy888, apooche
context: Discussion of unit cost notation and standardization for new player education.
